comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ all files listed in required_attachments (SDV §5.1 / §5.3 / §6, the continuation XML ea_decomposition EA-1 block, the three GOV tickets, and the primary anchor source files listed per-doc below). Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 121, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (in your own words, 3-5 sentences — must name all four deliverables and the STYLE.md-first ordering constraint) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence each for ALL work items WI-1 through WI-N — do not summarize or group; STYLE.md-first ordering must be explicit) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new governance doc + the STYLE.md sub-artifact + the ledger entry line) D. FILES TO MODIFY (enumerate: only docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md) E. FILES TO READ (per-doc anchoring list — every source file cited in the per-WI anchor lists in SECTION 4 below, plus the ADRs named per-doc) F. DELIVERABLE STRUCTURE (recite VERBATIM: branch name, every file name with its section headers as prescribed in STYLE.md's template, the ledger entry line, the expected commit message format) G. ORACLE EXPECTATION (recite VERBATIM the exact git diff --name-only output expected — only docs/governance/STYLE.md, docs/governance/pgov-validation.md, docs/governance/ipc-protocol.md, docs/governance/streaming-output.md, and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md) H. STYLE.md-FIRST ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote L-18 verbatim; confirm STYLE.md is authored and committed BEFORE you begin the three governance docs) I. CROSS-SPRINT COEXISTENCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT (L-16): quote verbatim — "Sprint 9 writes docs/governance/. Sprint 8 writes **/tests/. My writes respect this boundary." J. SOURCE-ANCHORING RECITATION (per SDV §6 / success criterion 6): for each of the three governance docs, recite: - the GOV ticket ID + Vikunja task number - the ADR(s) it will cite - at least one source file from the GOV ticket's "Scattered Sources" list STYLE.md does NOT require source anchoring — it is a meta-artifact. K. TARGET-AUDIENCE ASSIGNMENT (SDV §5.3): name the primary audience (operator / developer / auditor / incident responder / future agent) for each of the three governance docs. The "Audience" stanza opens each doc. L. 150-LINE FLOOR ACKNOWLEDGMENT (SDV §5.3 / §6): the three governance docs are each ≥ 150 lines of substantive content. STYLE.md is capped at ≤ 120 lines per L-18(c). M. RISKS AND AMBIGUITIES (list any — at minimum, note the parallel-with-Sprint-8 context, and whether source files have shifted since the GOV tickets were authored) N. PRODUCTION FILE PROHIBITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote L-15 verbatim)
STOP after posting the comprehension gate. Do NOT proceed to implementation. Wait for SDO VERDICT: APPROVED on Vikunja Task 121 before starting any work.

milestone.objective: Author the first four deliverables of the BlarAI governance documentation corpus under docs/governance/. The meta-artifact docs/governance/STYLE.md is authored FIRST and defines the shared template, section conventions, 150-line floor, and source-anchoring requirement that EA-2 through EA-5 will inherit. The three governance docs — pgov-validation.md (GOV-04), ipc-protocol.md (GOV-02), and streaming-output.md (GOV-03) — consolidate the security-boundary and wire-protocol knowledge currently scattered across production source code into a single auditor-accessible reference. This EA establishes the precedent for the rest of Sprint 9; every downstream EA attaches STYLE.md and follows its conventions.
milestone.ledger_entry_reserved: 56 (SDO projection; EA Code verifies next-free entry at commit time by scanning docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md for the highest existing ### Entry N and using N+1)
milestone.depends_on: main (HEAD at pickup time). No preceding Sprint 9 EA.
milestone.parallel_with: Sprint 8 (Task 82). Working sets disjoint per L-16: Sprint 8 = **/tests/; Sprint 9 = docs/governance/**.

work_item.title: Author docs/governance/STYLE.md — cross-EA coordination artifact
work_item.audience: future EA Code agents authoring Sprint 9 EA-2..EA-5; governance-doc maintainers
work_item.line_floor: NONE — STYLE.md is CAPPED at ≤ 120 lines per L-18(c). Keep it tight.
work_item.required_sections: STYLE.md MUST contain the following sections (verbatim header titles): # Governance Documentation Style Guide ## Purpose and Scope — State that STYLE.md is an internal coordination artifact, not a public contributor guide. It governs docs/governance/**.md authoring across Sprint 9 EA-1 through EA-5. ## Doc Template — Canonical section headers every governance doc uses (in order): # {Domain Name} Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites (ADR references, peer docs) ## Source References (file paths + line ranges where available) ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures (if applicable for the domain) ## Open Questions / Deferred Items — Note per-doc-type flex: not every doc has a "Recovery" section; auditor- facing docs may merge "Recovery" into "Governance Content" when a separate recovery section would be redundant. ## Line-Count Floor — 150 lines minimum of substantive content per governance doc. Short docs are a signal of incomplete thinking, not of a narrow topic. ## Source Anchoring — Every governance doc cites ≥ 1 ADR (where applicable) and ≥ 1 source file from its GOV ticket's "Scattered Sources" list. Cite files with repo-relative paths (e.g., services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py) and, where practical, with line ranges (e.g., lines 142-201). ## Audience Taxonomy — Enumerate: operator / developer / auditor / incident responder / future agent. Each doc names its primary audience in the "Audience" stanza. One doc MAY serve two audiences (list both) but SHOULD NOT serve all five (that is a signal the doc is too broad). ## Markdown Conventions — Headers: `#` for doc title, `##` for top-level sections, `###` for sub-sections. Do not go deeper than `####`. — Code blocks: triple-backtick-fenced; language-tagged where practical (`python`, `json`, `bash`). — Lists: `-` for bullets, `1.` for ordered. — Bold for verdicts/decisions (**ALLOWED**, **DENIED**, **REQUIRED**). — Tables for threshold/constant lookups. ## Cross-Doc References — Use relative links: `[peer doc](peer-doc.md)`. Do not hard-code absolute URLs. Do not reference docs/governance/boot-sequence.md — that is a phantom reference deferred to GOV-15 (Vikunja #124). ## Filename Conventions — lower-kebab-case, no `_GOVERNANCE` suffix (the `docs/governance/` directory itself supplies the governance context). — Examples: pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md. ## Out of Scope for docs/governance/ — Do not author ADRs here; ADRs live in their own location. — Do not modify docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md — deferred to GOV-MIGRATE (#123). — Do not author credentials-lifecycle.md or weight-integrity.md — Pluton-blocked pending ISS-4.
work_item.negative_constraints: - STYLE.md is for cross-EA coordination, not a public-facing contributor guide. Keep it ≤ 120 lines. (L-18(c) verbatim) - Do NOT include example governance-doc content inside STYLE.md — point to the canonical template instead. - Do NOT lock decisions that belong to later Co-Lead review (e.g., do not mandate a specific doc length beyond the 150-line floor, do not prescribe exact line counts for any section).
work_item.commit_checkpoint: Commit STYLE.md BEFORE beginning WI-2. Suggested separate commit: docs(task9/ea1): governance STYLE.md — ≤120 line cross-EA coordination artifact Rationale: if SDO or downstream reviewers need to reference STYLE.md during WI-2..WI-4 authoring, having it already committed removes ambiguity.

work_item.title: Author docs/governance/pgov-validation.md — PGOV Rules & Escalation Governance
work_item.anchor_sources: Required (must cite at least these two plus one ADR): - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (full implementation — read end-to-end; cite relevant line ranges for each of the six pipeline stages) - Use Cases_FINAL.md (Red Team ISSUE-005 — cite the ISSUE number and its acceptance framing) - ADR-012 §2.4 (thinking-mode strategy — PGOV interaction with AO streaming) Recommended additional (cite where it strengthens source anchoring): - docs/SECURITY_ASSESSMENT.md (PGOV-related findings; cite section title)
work_item.required_coverage: The doc MUST cover EACH of the following (minimum bar from GOV-04 ticket): 1. Six-stage pipeline specification (named in order): stage 1 — Token budget enforcement stage 2 — PII / secret detection (regex patterns; cite exact patterns from pgov.py or link to their location) stage 3 — Delimiter echo detection (exact strings: CONTEXT_BEGIN, CONTEXT_END, SYSTEM_BEGIN, SYSTEM_END) stage 4 — Tool-call allowlist enforcement (which tools permitted; where the allowlist is defined in source) stage 5 — Retrieval leakage detection (cosine-similarity threshold 0.85; the rationale for 0.85 per pgov.py or commit history) stage 6 — Final gate 2. Embedding model used for retrieval-leakage similarity (bge-small-en-v1.5 ONNX on CPU) 3. Fail-closed semantics: PGOV error == maximum leakage score == suppress. Document the exception path and what user sees. 4. Fallback message when PGOV denies (exact text shown to user) — cite the source location where that text lives. 5. Circuit-breaker interaction: if token budget fails, does the stream terminate or truncate? Document by reference to pgov.py behavior. 6. User notification of suppression: "output suppressed" banner vs. silent fallback — document observed behavior. 7. Audit trail: are PGOV denials logged? Where? Retention? 8. Threshold-tuning governance: evidence required before changing the 0.85 cosine threshold or the regex patterns. Reference Task 7 audit practice if applicable.
work_item.negative_constraints: - Do NOT author credentials-lifecycle content here (GOV-01 is Pluton-blocked). - Do NOT author weight-integrity content here (GOV-10 is Pluton-blocked). - Do NOT prescribe threshold changes — document current thresholds and the governance for changing them. - Do NOT forward-reference docs/governance/boot-sequence.md (phantom — L-17).

work_item.title: Author docs/governance/ipc-protocol.md — IPC Protocol & Message Format Governance
work_item.anchor_sources: Required (must cite at least these two plus one ADR): - shared/schemas/car.py (CAR dataclass definitions — cite each field with its type and purpose) - services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (StreamToken definition, transport layer — cite line ranges) - ADR-010 (Policy Agent on GPU — cites CAR usage context) OR an ADR that locks the CAR schema. If no ADR directly governs IPC, say so explicitly and cite the closest-relevant ADR. Recommended additional (cite where it strengthens source anchoring): - shared/ipc/protocol.py (protocol implementation) - shared/ipc/vsock.py (vsock transport) - services/policy_agent/src/ipc.py (PA listener)
work_item.required_coverage: The doc MUST cover EACH of the following (minimum bar from GOV-02 ticket): 1. Canonical Action Representation (CAR) schema: - JSON structure - required vs. optional fields - validation rules 2. CAR serialization / deserialization format 3. StreamToken structure: ALL fields including is_thinking (transport-layer bit added in Task 5 M3 per ADR-012 §2.4) 4. Request / response envelope format: - mTLS frame shape - JWT payload structure - CAR hash computation and placement 5. vsock wire protocol: - AF_HYPERV socket setup - CID and port constants (cite source) - TCP-like behavior notes 6. Message ordering guarantees (if any) — if none, state "none" explicitly. 7. Backpressure / flow-control semantics — if none, state "none" explicitly. 8. Error response format (schema returned on denial) 9. JWT-to-CAR hash verification at destination (who verifies; where in code) 10. Nonce field + replay detection at destination 11. Epoch field + epoch validation at destination 12. Example request/response cycles: - happy path (classify / allow / execute) - denial scenarios (DPC denial, PGOV denial) 13. Timeout behavior: sender wait SLA; receiver processing SLA
work_item.negative_constraints: - Do NOT propose schema changes. Document the current schema verbatim. - Do NOT write code examples that are not already in the codebase; cite source files and line ranges instead. - Do NOT author cross-references to docs/governance/boot-sequence.md (L-17).

work_item.title: Author docs/governance/streaming-output.md — Streaming Token Protocol & PGOV Handoff Governance
work_item.anchor_sources: Required (must cite at least these two plus one ADR): - services/ui_shell/src/streaming.py (StreamingDisplay — cite line ranges for buffer state machine) - services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py (PGOV validation — cite the specific stage(s) involved in streaming handoff) - ADR-012 §2.4 (thinking-mode strategy — PGOV: /no_think; AO: thinking allowed with strip) Recommended additional (cite where it strengthens source anchoring): - services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py (StreamToken transport layer) - Task 5 M3 prompt and execution reports (is_thinking transport-field addition) — cite the ledger entry or commit hash
work_item.required_coverage: The doc MUST cover EACH of the following (minimum bar from GOV-03 ticket): 1. StreamToken dataclass field semantics: token_string, confidence, is_thinking, source_model, timestamp (cite exact types and invariants for each) 2. Streaming lifecycle: first-token → mid-stream → final-token → end-of-sequence signal 3. TUI receives and buffers StreamToken(s) during AO generation: ordering, jitter handling, render cadence (cite streaming.py) 4. When PGOV validation occurs relative to streaming: after all tokens collected? before UI render? document observed behavior. 5. PGOV → TUI feedback loop if output is suppressed: what does the user see? which code path emits the suppression indicator? 6. Thinking-token handling in TUI: if is_thinking=True, how does display differ? strip vs. render-distinct? cite ADR-012 §2.4. 7. Buffer / state machine for StreamingDisplay in TUI (cite streaming.py) 8. Backpressure if UI can't render fast enough: does AO slow down? document the observed behavior. 9. Circuit-breaker integration: if token count hits MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS during streaming, how is the stream terminated? 10. Error states: if AO crashes mid-generation, what is the recovery path? (Forward-reference GOV-06 / error-recovery.md — not yet authored — is acceptable as a placeholder reference.)
work_item.negative_constraints: - Do NOT author GOV-06 error-recovery content inline; forward-reference only. - Do NOT document future TUI features (Sprint 9 is descriptive of current state, not prescriptive of future features). - Do NOT reference docs/governance/boot-sequence.md (L-17 phantom).

work_item.title: Add Sprint 9 EA-1 entry to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md
work_item.required_content:
Append a new ledger section with header: ### Entry N — Task 9 / EA-1: Sprint 9 Governance Documentation — Security Boundary & Wire Protocol
Where N = (highest existing ### Entry number in the ledger) + 1. SDO's projection is 56; EA Code MUST verify by scanning the ledger at commit time and using the next-free number regardless of projection.
Body MUST include: - Commit hash of the EA-1 branch merge (placeholder the branch HEAD if merge has not occurred at commit time; Co-Lead's merge commit supersedes). - Four deliverables authored with their file paths and approximate line counts (wc -l output). - STYLE.md-first protocol acknowledgment (L-18 satisfied). - Source-anchoring summary: ADR cites per doc + anchor-source files per doc. - Parallel-with-Sprint-8 note (L-16 coexistence). - Test-baseline confirmation: regression suite unchanged (expected: 755 passed, 2 skipped REGRESSION OR whatever the current baseline is at commit time — EA Code runs pytest and cites the exact observed count).

deliverable.files_to_create: - docs/governance/STYLE.md (≤ 120 lines, authored FIRST) - docs/governance/pgov-validation.md (≥ 150 lines) - docs/governance/ipc-protocol.md (≥ 150 lines) - docs/governance/streaming-output.md (≥ 150 lines)
deliverable.section_structure_per_doc: Every governance doc (WI-2, WI-3, WI-4) follows STYLE.md's Doc Template: # {Domain Name} Governance ## Audience ## Prerequisites ## Source References ## Governance Content ## Recovery / Remediation Procedures (if applicable — omit-or-merge per STYLE.md) ## Open Questions / Deferred Items The Audience stanza cites one of: operator / developer / auditor / incident responder / future agent. Source References cite ADRs and at least one source file from the GOV ticket's Scattered Sources list. Governance Content is the substantive body of the doc.

negative_constraints.constraint: NO file write outside docs/governance/ and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. Specifically PROHIBITED file paths for Sprint 9 EA-1: - **/tests/* (Sprint 8 territory) - shared/** (production code) - services/*/src/** (production code) - launcher/** (production code) - pyproject.toml - conftest.py (anywhere in the tree) - docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md (deferred to GOV-MIGRATE / Vikunja #123) - Any ADR file (docs reference ADRs; they do NOT modify them) If the diff shows any file in the above list, the ORACLE gate fails and the EA MUST halt and escalate to SDO via Task 121 comment before attempting fixes.

Cross-sprint boundary: Sprint 9 writes ONLY docs/governance/** (and the ledger). If during authoring you discover a test that would benefit from addition, do NOT add it — that is Sprint 8's territory. Document the observation in WI-5 ledger entry and move on.

Phantom-reference warning: docs/governance/boot-sequence.md does NOT exist and MUST NOT be authored in EA-1. If a source file you cite references a "boot sequence governance doc" that does not exist, cite the source file directly and footnote the gap — do NOT backfill by creating boot-sequence.md. That doc is deferred to GOV-15 (Vikunja #124). (Note: L-17 is informational for EA-1; the binding applies most directly to EA-2 and EA-4 which have more temptation paths. Included here defensively.)

STYLE.md is for cross-EA coordination, not a public-facing contributor guide. Keep STYLE.md ≤ 120 lines. It is authored FIRST, committed (optionally as its own commit) before the three governance docs begin.

Pluton-blocked content is out of scope: - GOV-01 Credentials & Certificate Lifecycle — do NOT author docs/governance/credentials-lifecycle.md in EA-1 - GOV-10 Weight Integrity Verification — do NOT author docs/governance/weight-integrity.md in EA-1 These remain blocked on ISS-4 Pluton investigation.

Parallel-sprint coexistence: Sprint 9 EA work MUST NOT reference Sprint 8 branches, test files, or commit hashes in the governance docs. The governance docs are timeless references to production code state, not sprint-execution logs. Ledger entry (WI-5) may mention the parallel-with-Sprint-8 context because the ledger IS a sprint-execution log.

quality_gate.gate: Every authored markdown file passes basic structural lint: - exactly one H1 (# ...) per doc - H2 section ordering matches STYLE.md's Doc Template (WI-2/3/4 only) - all fenced code blocks are closed - no broken relative links (manual check: every [text](file.md) references an existing file — at WI-4 completion, only STYLE.md and WI-2/WI-3 docs exist as valid link targets; forward-references to later docs in the corpus are FORBIDDEN) Command: eyeball each doc + `grep -E "^# " docs/governance/*.md` to confirm exactly one H1 per file.

Each of WI-2, WI-3, WI-4 cites: - at least 1 ADR (by ADR number) - at least 1 source file from its GOV ticket's Scattered Sources list Command: grep -E "ADR-[0-9]+" docs/governance/pgov-validation.md grep -E "services/|shared/" docs/governance/pgov-validation.md (repeat for ipc-protocol.md and streaming-output.md) Each command must return ≥ 1 match.
Each of WI-2, WI-3, WI-4 is ≥ 150 lines. STYLE.md (WI-1) is ≤ 120 lines. Command: wc -l docs/governance/*.md Expected: STYLE.md in [30, 120]; other three each ≥ 150.

Command: git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -vE "^docs/governance/|^docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER\.md$" Expected output: EMPTY (zero lines). If ANY file appears, STOP, post completion with the ORACLE failure, and await SDO guidance. Do NOT attempt auto-fixes.

Sprint 9 does not touch tests, but we confirm the regression baseline is unaffected: .venv/Scripts/pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q Expected: ≥ 755 passed, 2 skipped (REGRESSION baseline at SDO authoring time). If Sprint 8 EA-1 has merged to main before this EA commits, the baseline may have advanced to 777 passed, 2 skipped (per 1fb637f commit message). Report the observed count verbatim in the completion comment.

verification.instruction: After all WIs are committed and the five quality gates pass, post a completion comment on Vikunja Task 121 prefixed [agent:ea_code][phase:completion] with: - commit hash of the latest commit on the EA-1 branch - output of: git log --oneline main..HEAD - output of: git diff main...HEAD --name-only - output of: wc -l docs/governance/*.md - output of: pytest run summary (first/last lines) - a per-doc one-sentence synopsis + anchor-source confirmation - ORACLE gate result (PASS / FAIL; if FAIL, do NOT proceed) Apply Vikunja label: Gate:Pending-SDO (label_id 1 for Active remains; add label_id matching Gate:Pending-SDO — SDO will review Phase 1b on next cadence).
verification.terminal_commands: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git log --oneline -1 git diff main --name-only wc -l docs/governance/*.md .venv/Scripts/pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q
verification.expected_outputs: git diff main --name-only: docs/governance/STYLE.md docs/governance/ipc-protocol.md docs/governance/pgov-validation.md docs/governance/streaming-output.md docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md wc -l: STYLE.md ≤ 120; other three each ≥ 150. pytest summary: ≥ 755 passed, 2 skipped (or higher if Sprint 8 EA-1 has merged; cite exact observed counts).

ea_prompt.commit_template:
Final EA commit (after all WIs are authored and gates pass):
docs(task9/ea1): security + wire-protocol governance — 4 new docs (incl. STYLE.md), {L} lines total
- docs/governance/STYLE.md (cross-EA coordination artifact, L-18) - docs/governance/pgov-validation.md (GOV-04, HIGH — security boundary) - docs/governance/ipc-protocol.md (GOV-02, HIGH — cross-service comms) - docs/governance/streaming-output.md (GOV-03, HIGH — output path) - docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (Entry N — Task 9 / EA-1)
Sprint 9 parallel with Sprint 8 per L-16; ORACLE gate PASS. ADR cites: ADR-012 §2.4 (PGOV+streaming), ADR-010 (PA CAR context). Anchor sources: services/assistant_orchestrator/src/pgov.py, services/ui_gateway/src/transport.py, services/ui_shell/src/streaming.py, shared/schemas/car.py.
Ledger entry added: Entry N, Sprint 9 EA-1 Security Boundary & Wire Protocol.
Optional intermediate commit after STYLE.md only:
docs(task9/ea1): governance STYLE.md — ≤120-line cross-EA coordination artifact (L-18)

mature_not_minimal.line_floor: Each of pgov-validation.md, ipc-protocol.md, streaming-output.md: ≥ 150 lines of SUBSTANTIVE content (not including the YAML frontmatter or the H1 title line alone). Short docs are a signal that the required-coverage list has not been exhaustively addressed. If a doc comes in under 150 lines with ALL required_coverage items addressed, the doc is genuinely narrow and the EA MUST explicitly justify the deficit in the completion comment; this is a reviewable position, not an automatic failure.
mature_not_minimal.cross_referencing: Adjacent cross-referencing is permitted and encouraged: if while authoring streaming-output.md the EA discovers that ipc-protocol.md (already committed) would benefit from a forward-reference to streaming-output.md's PGOV-handoff section, the EA MAY include the forward-reference in streaming-output.md. The EA MUST NOT retroactively edit already-committed docs in this EA unless the edit is a pure link-text correction (no content shift). If a genuine content gap emerges in an already-committed doc, document it in WI-5 ledger entry and escalate to SDO for a follow-up EA patch.
mature_not_minimal.source_anchoring: Every governance doc cites ≥ 1 ADR and ≥ 1 source file with a repo-relative path. Where practical, cite line ranges (e.g., services/assistant_orchestrator/ src/pgov.py lines 142-201). Line-range citations age; path citations do not. Prefer path + section header (e.g., "pgov.py PGOVStageFive") over raw line numbers where the structure supports it.

required_attachments.attachment: docs/P5_TASK9_SDO_CONTINUATION_v1.0.xml (full — read ea_decomposition EA-1 block verbatim)
docs/adr/ (ADR-010, ADR-011, ADR-012 — whatever path hosts the ADRs; EA locates)
docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (for WI-5 — scan for highest ### Entry N)
required_attachments.note: STYLE.md is NOT in required_attachments here because WI-1 AUTHORS it. From EA-2 onward, STYLE.md IS in required_attachments.
